subject: Mathematics
title: Phi in Geometry
description: Learn Phi in Geometry in one minute.

hook.text: Draw a regular pentagon and connect all the diagonals. The golden ratio appears everywhere.

buildup.text: The diagonal of a regular pentagon divided by its side equals phi exactly.

discovery.text: A golden rectangle has sides in the ratio 1 to phi. Cut off a square and the remainder is another golden rectangle.

twist.text: This self-nesting property continues forever. It produces the famous golden spiral.

climax.text: The golden ratio hides in pentagrams, decagons, icosahedra, and other shapes tied to fivefold symmetry.

punchline.text: Wherever five-sided shapes appear, phi is nearby.

quiz.question: What happens when you cut a square from a golden rectangle?
